commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds guarded realtime tool-name filtering in the shared Talk consult resolver and in OpenAI Realtime and Google Live provider payload builders, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +167. Total +216 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source: current main sends custom realtime tool names directly into provider payloads, and the PR still permits Google names longer than the documented 64-character maximum. No live provider session was opened here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair to the Google provider regex and boundary test, while the protected maintainer label keeps merge and close decisions with maintainers.

target.title: fix(talk): quarantine malformed realtime tool names
target.body:
## Summary
- Skip unreadable, blank, or whitespace custom realtime tool names in the shared agent-consult tool resolver without dropping valid provider-specific names.
- Quarantine OpenAI realtime tools against the OpenAI name contract before WebSocket session updates and browser/WebRTC client-secret sessions.
- Quarantine Google Live function declarations against the Google function-name contract before connect config creation.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-realtime-tools-89175 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/talk/agent-consult-tool.ts src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.ts src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `blacksmith testbox list --all` blocked: not authenticated.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"` blocked before a `tbx_...` id: delegated Blacksmith warmup reported not authenticated.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "true"` selected provider `azure` and blocked before a lease id: coordinator `/v1/runs` and `/v1/leases` returned HTTP 401 unauthorized.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: malformed custom realtime voice tool names no longer crash or poison realtime provider session payload construction, while valid provider-specific OpenAI and Google names stay available.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest wrapper shards for the shared Talk helper, OpenAI realtime provider, and Google realtime provider on final head `88abeb6c2531625a85e7c84fe1e958199afcf2f0`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest command above; targeted oxfmt; targeted oxlint; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; branch autoreview against `origin/main`; remote runner auth preflights above.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 shards / 83 tests; targeted formatter, lint, and diff checks passed; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings and `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`.

Observed result after fix: unreadable and malformed realtime tool names are skipped before OpenAI WebSocket, OpenAI browser/WebRTC, and Google Live provider payloads are built; OpenAI-valid `1_lookup` and Google-valid `calendar.lookup:next` remain exposed on their respective provider paths.

What was not tested: remote `check:changed` and live OpenAI/Google realtime sessions were not run because Blacksmith auth is missing and Crabbox coordinator calls return HTTP 401 in this environment.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
